records.ety: [OE. glorie, OF. glorie, gloire, F. gloire, fr. L. gloria; prob. akin to Gr. kle`os, Skr. çravas glory, praise, çru to hear. See Loud.]

defs: Praise, honor, admiration, or distinction, accorded by common consent to a person or thing; high reputation; honorable fame; renown.
quotes: Glory to God in the highest.
Spread his glory through all countries wide.

defs: That quality in a person or thing which secures general praise or honor; that which brings or gives renown; an object of pride or boast; the occasion of praise; excellency; brilliancy; splendor.
quotes: Think it no glory to swell in tyranny.
Jewels lose their glory if neglected.
Your sex's glory 't is to shine unknown.

defs: The presence of the Divine Being; the manifestations of the divine nature and favor to the blessed in heaven; celestial honor; heaven.
quotes: Thou shalt guide me with thy counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.

defs: An emanation of light supposed to proceed from beings of peculiar sanctity. It is represented in art by rays of gold, or the like, proceeding from the head or body, or by a disk, or a mere line.
notes: ☞ This is the general term; when confined to the head it is properly called nimbus; when encircling the whole body, aureola or aureole.

records.ety: [OE. glorien, OF. glorier, fr. L. gloriari, fr. gloria glory. See Glory, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gloried (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Glorying.]

defs: To exult with joy; to rejoice.
quotes: Glory ye in his holy name.
God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.
No one . . . should glory in his prosperity.
